_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot)
_meta.note: Atlanta GA local batch 3 — City Council President Overstreet + councilmembers Norwood (D8), Westmoreland (AL-2), Bond (AL-1), Worthy (D7). Evidence-based finalization via 5 parallel research agents; roster/currency verified June 2026 (all sworn in Jan 5, 2026 after the Nov/Dec 2025 municipal elections). reset_unspecified clears prior speculative party-default cells; FALSE only where a documented vote/statement/sponsored measure/single-issue endorsement exists; null where no evidence (not penalized).

profile.background: Atlanta native; B.A. journalism, Georgia State; Delta Air Lines flight attendant 1990-2009; small-business co-founder. Atlanta City Council District 11 member 2018-2026; elected citywide Council President Nov 2025, sworn in Jan 5, 2026 (term to Jan 2030).

marci-collier-overstreet.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Currency verified: won the citywide Council President race Nov 4, 2025, sworn in Jan 5, 2026 (Ballotpedia; Rough Draft Atlanta 1/5/2026; official council page). HARD pro-police record: voted YES on the Public Safety Training Center authorization (Sept 2021) and its $67M funding (June 2023), campaigned as its 'strong advocate,' and says she wants 'a well-trained police department' (public_justice q0 TRUE). HARD contrary evidence on immigration: touts Atlanta as a welcoming city that 'deactivated' ICE cooperation and says 'No one should be criminalized just because they weren't born here' (ATL Press Collective Q&A, June 2025 — border q1/q2 FALSE). At a Sept 2025 LGBTQ+ candidate forum she backed mandatory ongoing LGBTQ+ sensitivity training for APD ('Unequivocally, yes') and urged continued HOPWA funding (biblical_marriage q4 FALSE). Co-authored the city Inspector General legislation (2020, anti-corruption) — noted, but not a deficit/balanced-budget position, so economic q2 left null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, marriage definition, trans ideology per se, schools/parental rights, religious liberty, elections administration, state-overreach resistance, or guns — those cells are null, not penalized. No public faith affiliation found. Sparse-cell caution: 4 scored cells.

4.note: At the Sept 30, 2025 Atlanta LGBTQ+ candidates forum, backed mandatory ongoing LGBTQ+ sensitivity training for APD: 'Unequivocally, yes… it's absolutely paramount that that training is included at the Public Safety Training Center'; also urged continued HOPWA progress — affirmative support for LGBTQ+-identity policy, the documented contrary of opposing it.

0.note: Affirmative advocacy beyond routine budgets: voted YES on the Public Safety Training Center lease (Sept 2021) and $67M funding (June 2023); self-described 'strong advocate' for the center in the Oct 2025 race; 'Public Safety is directly tied to the quality of life of our citizens.' No defund advocacy found.

1.note: June 2025 Q&A: 'No one should be criminalized just because they weren't born here in Atlanta or the United States' — documented contrary of supporting mandatory deportation.

2.note: Touts Atlanta as a welcoming city that 'deactivated' ICE cooperation — supports, not opposes, sanctuary-style non-cooperation.

profile.background: Independent; Atlanta City Council at-large 2002-2010 and 2017-2021, District 8 (Buckhead) since 2021; re-elected unopposed Nov 2025 (term to Jan 2030). Two-time mayoral runner-up (2009, 2017).

mary-norwood.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Currency verified: holds District 8, re-elected unopposed Nov 4, 2025, 2026 committee assignments received (official council bio; Ballotpedia; Fox 5 Jan 2026). HARD fiscal restraint: voted AGAINST the June 2009 42% property-tax increase and a June 2008 tax-hike proposal, 'insisting there was money to be found within Atlanta's budget'; long record pushing fiscal accountability/the Inspector General (economic q2 TRUE). HARD pro-police: voted YES and co-sponsored the June 2023 Public Safety Training Center funding (11-4), called the facility 'a wonderful program,' sponsored the Buckhead Public Safety Task Force (Jan 2022), wrote the 2019 open letter demanding a crime plan (public_justice q0 TRUE). Social-issue contrary evidence: reported pro-abortion-rights stance (The Intercept 2017 — q0 FALSE, secondhand characterization, no first-person quote located); supported same-sex marriage legalization and rallied against Georgia's marriage ban, with HARD 2017 single-issue endorsements from Georgia Equality and Log Cabin Republicans (biblical_marriage q0/q4 FALSE). Election integrity: requested a full hand recount of paper ballots + photo-ID verification of 92k voter certificates after her 832-vote 2017 loss, and signed a Nov 2020 affidavit supporting Pearson v. Kemp citing signature-match weaknesses — documented in notes but situational rather than a stated policy on machines/voter-ID law, so election cells left null. Deliberately neutral on Buckhead cityhood ('I have not taken a position'). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on schools/parental rights, religious liberty, immigration, guns, trans policy, or personal faith — null.

0.note: The Intercept (Dec 2017): her stances 'supporting abortion rights and marriage equality' placed her out of step with the GOP — reported, uncontested characterization of a pro-abortion-rights stance; the documented contrary of affirming personhood from conception. No first-person quote located (soft-sourced; q1 left null rather than double-counting).

Voiced support for legalizing same-sex marriage in her 2009 run and rallied against Georgia's marriage ban; accepted Georgia Equality's 2017 runoff endorsement (hard single-issue endorsement) — documented contrary of affirming one-man-one-woman marriage.

4.note: Georgia Equality and Log Cabin Republicans endorsements (2017) plus Atlanta Pride participation — aligned with, not opposed to, LGBTQ+ promotion in public policy.

2.note: Voted against the June 2009 property-tax increase (7.12→10.12 mills) and the June 2008 tax-hike proposal, 'insisting there was money to be found within Atlanta's budget'; sustained fiscal-accountability record credited in creating Atlanta's first independent Inspector General.

0.note: Affirmative advocacy beyond routine budgets: YES vote + co-sponsorship of the June 2023 Public Safety Training Center funding (11-4), called the facility 'a wonderful program'; sponsored the 21-member Buckhead Public Safety Task Force (Jan 2022); 2019 open letter demanding a mayoral crime plan.

profile.religion: United Methodist (lifelong member, Peachtree Road UMC)
profile.background: Princeton graduate; Carver High School history teacher; Atlanta Board of Education District 3 (2014-2017); Atlanta City Council At-Large Post 2 since 2018, re-elected unopposed Nov 2025.

matt-westmoreland.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Currency verified: ran unopposed Nov 4, 2025 for a third term at At-Large Post 2 (Atlanta Civic Circle qualified-candidates list; Ballotpedia; official council profile). HARD contrary evidence on life: co-sponsored Resolution 22-R-3711 (2022) directing that no city funds be used to record/investigate abortion reports and that APD treat them as lowest priority (sanctity q0/q1 FALSE). HARD LGBTQ record: came out as gay Jan 2022 ('a proud member of our LGBTQ+ community'); sponsored 2023 legislation funding the city's 'single largest investment in the transgender community to date' ($55K: name-change aid, LGBTQ mentoring) (biblical_marriage q2/q4 FALSE). HARD pro-police-infrastructure: YES on the 2021 training-center lease (10-4 roll call) and YES + co-sponsor on the June 2023 $31M+ funding (11-4); champions 'historic 30 percent pay raises' for APD/Fire — while calling protester domestic-terrorism charges excessive (public_justice q0 TRUE). Lifelong Peachtree Road UMC member who ties his public-service path to his church (faith recorded in profile; not scored as a religious-liberty policy position). On charters (2017 AJC guide): 'We have not authorized a single new charter school in four years,' favoring district-led turnaround — non-expansion statement, left null rather than over-read as opposition. Opposed Buckhead cityhood (participated in Neighbors for a United Atlanta kickoff, Dec 2021) — intra-local, not state-mandate resistance, so refuse_state_overreach left null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on elections, guns under his name, COVID mandates, parental notification/CRT, or immigration roll calls attributable to him — null.

0.note: Co-sponsored Resolution 22-R-3711 (2022) requesting no city funds be used to record/investigate abortion-care reports and that APD place them at lowest priority — a sponsored pro-abortion-access measure, the documented contrary of affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Same sponsored measure shields abortion access from enforcement — the documented contrary of advocating abortion abolition.

2.note: Sponsored 2023 legislation funding the city's 'single largest investment in the transgender community to date' ($55K: legal name-change aid, LGBTQ mentoring, peer-support scholarships) — documented affirmation of transgender identity policy, the contrary of rejecting it.

4.note: Sponsored the trans-community funding package and publicly identifies as 'a proud member of our LGBTQ+ community' (Jan 2022) — promotes, does not oppose, LGBTQ+ identity in public policy.

0.note: Affirmative advocacy beyond routine budgets: YES on the Sept 2021 training-center lease (roll call) and YES + co-sponsor on the June 2023 funding after ~14 hours of opposed comment; champions 'historic 30 percent pay raises' for police and fire. Not a defund voice.

profile.religion: Baptist (30+ year member, Antioch Baptist Church North — self-stated)
profile.background: Son of civil-rights leader Julian Bond. Atlanta City Council District 3 1993-2001; At-Large Post 1 since 2009; won a 5th at-large term outright Nov 4, 2025 (58.5%, no runoff).

michael-julian-bond.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Currency verified: defeated Mendoza and Rinker Nov 4, 2025, remains At-Large Post 1 (Ballotpedia; Axios Atlanta 11/5/2025; official council page). HARD pro-police: voted FOR the Sept 2021 training-center lease ordinance and the June 2023 funding (11-4), and vocally defended the center against 'Cop City' backlash — 'There are over 3,000 911 calls a day in Atlanta, so there's a demonstrated need for this service' (public_justice q0 TRUE). Faith in the public square: self-stated 30+ year member of Antioch Baptist Church North; co-hosted the city's 'People's Power of Prayer' event with Hosea Helps (2020, official press release) (christian_liberty q3 TRUE). Bail evidence MIXED, left null: sponsored 21-R-3261 to review-but-explicitly-NOT-repeal Atlanta's 2018 cash-bail elimination (contrary signal) yet earlier proposed denying signature bail for street-racing arrests (aligned signal); also sponsored the 22-O-1774 ACDC jail-bed lease to the Fulton sheriff. Marched/danced in the Atlanta Pride parade — public participation, not a policy act, so biblical_marriage q4 left null with this note. Sponsored the 2026 TAD extensions to 2056 (tax-increment diversion — noted, not a deficit-spending cell). ATTRIBUTION TRAP documented: the March-April 2026 anti-ICE-detention resolutions were sponsored by Kelsea Bond (District 2), NOT Michael Julian Bond — not attributed. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, marriage/trans policy, schools/parents, elections, guns — null. Sparse-cell caution: only 2 scored cells; headline % rests on a thin denominator.

3.note: Co-hosted 'The People's Power of Prayer' public prayer event with Hosea Helps honoring C.T. Vivian and John Lewis (official city press release, 2020) — documented support for prayer in the public square as an officeholder; self-stated 30+ year Antioch Baptist Church North member.

0.note: Affirmative advocacy beyond routine budgets: voted FOR the Sept 2021 lease (21-O-0367) and June 2023 funding; publicly defended the center through the backlash — 'over 3,000 911 calls a day… demonstrated need for this service' — despite threats against his office.

1.note: MIXED evidence, not scored: sponsored 21-R-3261 to review but explicitly NOT repeal the 2018 cash-bail-elimination ordinance (contrary signal), yet proposed 20-O-1653 denying signature bail for street-racing arrests and sponsored the 22-O-1774 ACDC jail-bed lease to the Fulton sheriff (aligned signals).

4.note: Marched/danced in the Atlanta Pride parade as a councilman — public participation, but no sponsored measure or policy statement located, so not scored as a policy position.

profile.background: Attorney (Washington & Lee BA, UVA Law); led Georgia justice-reform efforts 2012-16; Chief Public Policy Officer, Piedmont Healthcare; MARTA Board 2017-2025 (chairman). Won the Dec 2, 2025 District 7 runoff 59.3%-40.7% (succeeding Howard Shook); sworn in Jan 5, 2026.

thomas-worthy.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Currency verified: Nov 4, 2025 general went to a runoff (Worthy 27.1% vs Flowers 26.4%); won the Dec 2, 2025 runoff 59.3%, sworn in Jan 5, 2026, term to Jan 2030 (Ballotpedia; Atlanta Civic Circle 1/6/2026; Center for Civic Innovation). New member — no council roll calls yet; scored from his own campaign statements, questionnaires, and endorsements per the social-media protocol. HARD fiscal: 'I believe we can deliver high-quality city services while keeping taxes low'; proposed updating decades-old permit fees 'to generate revenue without raising tax rates'; praised TADs that 'sunset when appropriate to protect the city's general fund' and MARTA 'budget discipline… preserving a AAA bond rating' (economic q2 TRUE; also supports a new dedicated stormwater fee — noted). HARD pro-police: calls the Public Safety Training Center 'one of the most important investments Atlanta has made in the future of public safety'; sits on the Atlanta Police Foundation Board; endorsed by IBPO Local 623 and Atlanta Professional Firefighters Local 134 (public_justice q0 TRUE). Reform-minded on incarceration ('reserved for violent offenders… not a substitute for mental health care') and co-chaired the bipartisan GA Council on Criminal Justice Reform — mixed mapping, public_justice q1 left null. Local self-governance: 'Atlanta must stand firm against federal overreach… defend Atlanta's right to govern itself' (Oct 2025) — affirmation of decisions at the local level (refuse_state_overreach q3 TRUE; federal-not-state context noted honestly). HARD gun-control alignment: accepted the Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' designation; campaign says he 'advocates for gun violence prevention' (self_defense q1 FALSE; q0 null — no carry-specific statement). Endorsement coalition (Mayor Dickens, Sierra Club, Abundant Housing, MDA, police/fire unions) is Democrat-aligned but inference, not scored. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, marriage/LGBTQ, school choice/parental rights, religious liberty, elections, immigration/ICE, or personal faith — null.

2.note: Own platform: 'deliver high-quality city services while keeping taxes low'; update decades-old fees 'to generate revenue without raising tax rates'; praised TADs that 'sunset when appropriate to protect the city's general fund' and MARTA 'budget discipline… preserving a AAA bond rating.' (Also supports a new dedicated stormwater utility fee — noted for balance.)

0.note: Affirmative advocacy: calls the Public Safety Training Center 'one of the most important investments Atlanta has made in the future of public safety'; serves on the Atlanta Police Foundation Board; endorsed by the police union (IBPO Local 623) and firefighters Local 134.

1.note: Mixed mapping, not scored: 'Incarceration should be reserved for violent offenders who pose a true threat to safety, not a substitute for mental health care, addiction treatment or poverty intervention'; co-chaired Georgia's bipartisan Council on Criminal Justice Reform (2011-16 reforms) — reform posture, but no bail/prosecutor-specific position.

3.note: Oct 2025: 'Atlanta must stand firm against federal overreach that threatens our local authority or values. I'll work with the mayor and city attorney to defend Atlanta's right to govern itself' — an affirmation of decisions made at the lowest competent level (context is federal, not state, overreach; noted honestly).

1.note: Accepted the Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' designation (hard single-issue endorsement); campaign states he 'advocates for gun violence prevention and will govern with gun safety in mind' — alignment with the gun-control agenda, the documented contrary of opposing red-flag laws/bans.
